title: Mota Flash Game Reproduction in RPG Maker XP
summary: Reproduce the first three floors of a classic Flash "Mota" (魔塔) tower-defense RPG inside RPG Maker XP — rebuild the maps, monsters, and combat logic, then play through and capture milestone screenshots on every floor reached.
taskPrompt:
Goal: Reproduce a flash game with RPGMaker.

Before you begin:
1. Open the original game `mota_24\input\mota-24.swf` in Ruffle yourself — e.g. double-click in Explorer, or run `Start-Process 'mota_24\input\mota-24.swf'` in PowerShell. Use full screen for best visibility.
2. Copy the template project folder `mota_24\input\mota_template` to `mota_24\output\mota_template` — this is your working copy. PowerShell: `Copy-Item -Path 'mota_24\input\mota_template' -Destination 'mota_24\output' -Recurse`
3. Open `mota_24\output\mota_template\Game.rxproj` in RPGMakerXP — e.g. double-click in Explorer, or run `Start-Process 'mota_24\output\mota_template\Game.rxproj'`.

Then reproduce the game from level 1 - 3 including game map, monsters, fighting logics.
 
Output:
After completing the reproduction:
- Preview the reproduced game in RPGMakerXP.
- Play through the game and successfully navigate to each new floor.
- Upon reaching each floor, save a milestone screenshot using: `save_milestone_screenshot(path="mota_24\output\$FLOOR_NUMBER$.png")`, where $FLOOR_NUMBER$ is the floor number you reached.

Verification: 
The task is considered successful if:
- The reproduced game allows the same player operations as the original game.
- The player can reach the same game states (map layout, player status) as in the original game.
- The reproduced game functions correctly when run as an EXE, with no blocking issues in progression from Level 0 (the prologue if exists) to 3.
agentMustDo: Open the original `input/mota-24.swf` Flash game and inspect each floor's map layout, monster set, and combat behavior.
Open the RPG Maker XP template project at `input/mota_template` (copied into `output/mota_template` during setup).
Rebuild floors 1 – 3 in RPG Maker XP: map tiles, NPC / monster placement, item placement, and fight rules.
Play the reproduced game inside RPG Maker XP and use `save_milestone_screenshot(path="output/<floor>.png")` on every floor reached.
Ensure the project still runs end-to-end from Level 0 through Level 3 without blocking bugs.

inputFiles.name: mota-24.swf
inputFiles.description: Original Flash build of the Mota game — visual / behavioral reference.

inputFiles.name: mota_template/
inputFiles.description: RPG Maker XP starter project pre-staged for the agent; copied into `output/mota_template/` at setup.

referenceFiles.name: Per-floor milestone screenshots
referenceFiles.description: Milestone screenshots captured during reproduced gameplay; one PNG per floor reached, file stems must match the reference set.

evaluation: Evaluator collects matching screenshot files between `output/` and the reference set, then for each match runs three LLM-vision yes/no judgments comparing the reproduced screenshot to the reference: (1) whether the reproduction is actually in RPG Maker XP (signaled by the orange sun-like icon in the top-left), (2) whether the map layout matches the original, and (3) whether player status matches. Per-screenshot score is `0.5 * map_score + 0.5 * player_score`, gated on the RPG-Maker check; the final task score is the aggregate across all reference floors. Pass threshold (documented 2026-06-02): final aggregate >= 0.5 — empirically the negative fixture scores 0.25 and a faithful reproduction scores ~1.0 (judge characterization: RPG-Maker gate + map-match + player-match each verified to discriminate correctly). Requires a valid OpenAI key in secret/eval_time/openai.env (the vision judge silently scores 0 on a 401).

evaluatorCredentials.purpose: OpenAI vision/LLM judge scores the deliverable during evaluate(); sourced from secret/eval_time/openai.env

classifier.rationale: Reconstructing a Flash game in RPG Maker XP is interactive-media / game authoring work.
